items.rationale: Core thesis of his book and ongoing work: NFTs let brands give customers ownership/identity stakes, bridge physical-digital goods, and co-create with communities; he sees mainstream brand adoption (Starbucks, Nike, Reddit) scaling via low-cost L2/custodial collectibles.

citations.quote: NFTs are poised to become ubiquitous brand assets in 2024.
having started out by addressing a side market, NFTs are now starting to compete with standard branded collectibles

items.rationale: Believes a programmable IP layer (linking on-chain licensing to real-world IP regimes) can fix long-standing IP market failures, made more urgent by generative-AI remixing/attribution problems; he is leading a16z's Story Protocol Series B, his single strongest stated excitement.

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol.
I'm extremely excited see what having a programmable onchain IP layer can unlock.

items.rationale: Sees user-controlled, portable assets/wallets shifting network effects from walled-garden platforms to assets and protocols, intensifying competition (Five Forces) but making value creation less zero-sum through composability and embeddedness.

citations.quote: Web3 has the potential to disrupt the monolithic power of today's top web platforms.
In web3, competitive advantage is powered by collaboration.

items.rationale: Repeatedly argues open protocols (vs. proprietary platforms) are needed for portable follow-graphs/data and to inject competition into social media; ties to his HBR work on interoperability.

citations.quote: The need for competition in the social media market is a big part of why I'm excited about decentralized social media protocols

items.rationale: Treats tokens as monetary systems requiring adaptive design; warns against fixing supply/emissions up front (the Bitcoin precedent) and favors evolving monetary policy as money demand changes — a market-design lens he applies to protocols and NFT sales.

citations.quote: With token design, monetary policy evolving over time should be the default.

items.rationale: Views on-chain games as ideal settings for mechanism/incentive design; active advisor/player (Proof of Play, fragment/MegaForce, Project Shinsei).
items.age_note: Strongest explicit statement is 2023-09-21; sustained through 2024 only via project involvement, not restated thesis.

citations.quote: On-chain games are a perfect setting (and laboratory) for incentive design

items.rationale: Long-standing interest (dating to work with Gary Becker) in sharing returns from medical research with data contributors; sees blockchain solving infrastructure/access barriers that stalled prior health-data market designs (Amino Chain).

citations.quote: I've been fascinated by health data market design ever since.

items.rationale: Considers royalties a key NFT value proposition but flags persistent implementation/enforcement difficulty; co-authored a piece reviewing trade-offs and proposing new approaches — an open, unsolved problem he is wary of.

citations.quote: Creator royalties are a key value proposition of NFTs, but they've faced significant implementation challenges.

items.rationale: Repeatedly argues that ad hoc enforcement harms innovation and consumers; favors clear, principles-based rules and actively backs legislation (FIT21) — concern is the current regime, not the technology.

citations.quote: FIT21 is a huge step forward for blockchain and crypto technology, QED.

items.rationale: Critiques supply/demand imbalances in token sales (e.g., the Azuki Elementals SBT episode) that produce gas wars and bad outcomes; sees mechanism design as undervalued by projects.
items.age_note: Most vivid statements are mid-2023 (Azuki); not strongly restated near 2024-09-30, though consistent with his ongoing market-design framing.

citations.quote: fundamentally gas wars are about supply/demand (im)balance

items.rationale: Dismissive of memecoins as low-substance, viral-by-replication assets; contrasts them implicitly with the consumption/utility/IP value he thinks drives durable crypto value.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish on crypto/web3/NFTs as transformative infrastructure, repeatedly analogizing the moment to the early internet (~1997) and actively leading investments (Story Protocol, Amino Chain) and writing/teaching to advance the space. His optimism is tempered, not contradicted: he stresses the tech is immature, many business models are unsustainable or fraudulent, and value is broader than token prices (he calls over-financialization 'overblown'). Net posture is long-horizon risk-on with a builder's, not a speculator's, framing.

notes: Corpus is dominated by Wordle scores, NFT-community in-jokes, math/number facts, and Covid-mitigation advocacy — none of which are market views and were excluded. Kominers expresses views as a market designer/investor at a16z crypto, not as a token picker: he names almost no individual cryptocurrencies as buys, so tokens_excited is empty rather than fabricated. His excitement clusters around infrastructure and brand/identity/IP use-cases (NFTs-as-brands, programmable IP, decentralized protocols) rather than price speculation; his concerns are about design/enforcement gaps and regulatory process, plus dismissal of memecoins. He consistently downplays the 'it's all a scam / over-financialized' critique, instead framing crypto as immature-but-promising infrastructure. All views are recent (mostly 2024) and mutually consistent; no major reversals were found — the only aging stances are on-chain games (peak 2023, sustained via involvement) and NFT-mint mechanism critiques (mid-2023).
